hero.kicker: 2 independent AI coding agents · same app · same tasks · 60 real runs
hero.headline: Both coding agents did measurably less database work on MongoDB.
hero.statement: Both agents independently read more context, cost more, and took longer on Postgres for the same tasks. Retries was mixed across agents — see per-agent detail.

claimLabel: 3 schema shapes × 2 agents × 5 repeats = 60 real runs

agreement.statement: Both agents independently read more context, cost more, and took longer on Postgres for the same tasks. Retries was mixed across agents — see per-agent detail.

databaseVerdict.caveat: Within-agent comparison only. Cross-agent absolute numbers are not comparable (CLIs report tokens differently; tokensRead = inputTokens + cachedInputTokens).

shapeVerdict.caveat: Within-agent comparison only. Same business outcome across all shapes; only Postgres relational depth differs.

mongo.summary: Agent read the document directly (19 reads), no joins.

whyHighlights.traceNote: Heuristic indicators parsed from the raw agent transcript (table reads, JOIN mentions, FK errors). Directional, not an exact instruction count.

mongo.summary: Agent read the document directly (20 reads), no joins.

postgres.summary: Agent inspected ~24 tables, wrote 26 JOINs, hit 1 FK errors.

whyHighlights.traceNote: Heuristic indicators parsed from the raw agent transcript (table reads, JOIN mentions, FK errors). Directional, not an exact instruction count.

mongo.summary: Agent read the document directly (23 reads), no joins.

whyHighlights.traceNote: Heuristic indicators parsed from the raw agent transcript (table reads, JOIN mentions, FK errors). Directional, not an exact instruction count.

methodology.withinAgentOnly: Within-agent comparison only. Each agent is compared to itself across databases; cross-agent absolute token counts are never compared (the CLIs report tokens differently).
methodology.tokenMetric: Token metric = context tokens read = inputTokens + cachedInputTokens.
methodology.sameOutcome: All 3 schema shapes implement the SAME business outcome with ONE shared acceptance contract. Only the Postgres relational depth differs, so any metric difference is attributable to schema shape alone.
methodology.idiomaticPostgres: Every Postgres schema is idiomatic best-practice normalization (sensible keys, a real M:N junction in the deep shape), not a contrived strawman.
methodology.fullV1Scope: A full public-V1 benchmark would be 25 tasks × 2 lanes × 3 agents × 3 repeats = 450 runs. This focused MVP spends the same ~60-run budget on the causal variable (schema depth) instead of repetition.
methodology.pathToOfficial: Path to officially-endorsed material: an independent Postgres-fairness review and MongoDB brand/legal sign-off. Both are out of scope for this pilot and documented as next steps.

evidenceClaims.label: What was run?
evidenceClaims.question: What was run?
evidenceClaims.verdict: AST-Bench v2: 3 schema shapes × 2 agents × 2 lanes × 5 repeats = 60 required runs. 60 passed so far.

evidenceClaims.label: Are the 3 shapes genuinely different?
evidenceClaims.question: Are the 3 shapes genuinely different?
evidenceClaims.verdict: Deep has 17+ normalized Postgres tables (including a real M:N junction). Shallow collapses owner_groups and risk_signals into pipe-delimited columns. Moderate is standard 12-table normalization. MongoDB is a single document for all shapes.

evidenceClaims.label: Are the runs real and measured?
evidenceClaims.question: Are the runs real and measured?
evidenceClaims.verdict: 60 lane runs captured. Tokens measured from raw transcripts. cheatSignals enforced per run (shape=deep, agent=claude-code, repeat=1).

evidenceClaims.label: Can I see what the agent actually did?
evidenceClaims.question: Can I see what the agent actually did?
evidenceClaims.verdict: Raw agent transcript captured for shape=deep, agent=claude-code, repeat=1. Both lanes present and hashed.

evidenceClaims.label: How is the verdict computed (no cross-agent trick)?
evidenceClaims.question: How is the verdict computed (no cross-agent trick)?
evidenceClaims.verdict: Each agent is scored against itself: mongo lane vs postgres lane for the same agent, shape, and repeat. No cross-agent absolute token counts are compared.

evidenceClaims.label: Can the page overclaim or fake data?
evidenceClaims.question: Can the page overclaim or fake data?
evidenceClaims.verdict: benchmark-run.mjs enforces cheatSignals checks per cell. check-no-mock-data.mjs blocks any mock or hardcoded data from entering the bundle.

evidenceClaims.label: Is Postgres treated fairly?
evidenceClaims.question: Is Postgres treated fairly?
evidenceClaims.verdict: Every Postgres schema uses idiomatic normalization per the v2 spec. The deep shape includes a real M:N junction table, not a contrived strawman. Both databases receive the same acceptance contract.

caveats: Within-agent comparison only. Cross-agent absolute token counts are not comparable.
Same business outcome across all 3 schema shapes; only Postgres relational depth differs.
A MongoDB loss on any metric or shape is shown, not hidden.
Idiomatic Postgres normalization; independent fairness review is the documented path to official status.
